title: Spicy Crumbly Argentinian Beef Empanada
description: A traditional Argentinian dish, crumbly beef empanada with a spicy twist, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: In a large pan, over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the diced onion to the pan and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chili flakes, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook until the mixture has thickened slightly.
Add the chopped parsley to the pan and stir to combine.
Roll out the empanada pastry and cut out 12 circles.
Spoon a tablespoon of the beef mixture onto one half of each pastry circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the pastry in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanada on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with egg for a golden glaze.
Bake the empanada for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
